Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests and update plan doc for Phase 2 parseRawAliasTargetTemplateId

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/4d6729f5-2f4d-4ba9-b719-bf3af8c367e4

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests/test_member_alias_template_global_scope_ret42.cpp

Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests/test_alias_template_global_scope_ret42.cpp

Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: don't include leading :: in captured alias target name

The template registry stores alias templates without the :: prefix,
so including it in the captured name causes lookup failures during
deferred alias materialization. The class alias path has no
lookup_alias_template guard, so a ::prefixed name passes the
isValid() check but fails at materialization time.

Skip the :: token without appending it to the name builder.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Gregor Gullwi <gregor.gullwi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ozone): phase 1G — frontend-choice on-ramp to ozone+

Add an interactive FrontendChoice screen to the ozone launcher so users
can pick SillyTavern or ozone+ after confirming a launch plan:

- Add Screen::FrontendChoice and FrontendMode (SillyTavern | OzonePlus)
  to src/ui/mod.rs; screen sits between Confirm and Launching
- Add render_frontend_choice() to src/ui/launcher.rs — centred list
  with ↑↓ navigation, Enter to confirm, Esc to go back
- Add --frontend <MODE> CLI flag to ozone binary (sillyTavern | ozonePlus)
  to bypass the interactive screen from scripts/CI
- Backend (KoboldCpp) is always started first regardless of choice
- ozone+ path: disable_raw_mode + LeaveAlternateScreen, then execv() to
  ozone-plus list so the terminal is clean and no subprocess overhead
- SillyTavern path: unchanged (open browser, enter Monitor screen)
- Update .mex/ROUTER.md to mark Phase 1 complete
- Add .mex/patterns/ozoneplus-phase1g-launcher-onramp.md

All cargo gates green: check, 6+8 tests, clippy -D warnings (both crates).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: enrich README news and section descriptions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

rem: close challenger criticals — full header auth + atomic write + event/flag validation

- SHA-256 now covers zeroed-header+schema+spirv+payload (was schema+spirv+payload only)
  Closes header metadata tamper gap: event_count, flags, and length fields are now authenticated
- compile(): atomic write via tmp-pid file + rename (no truncated stones on crash/kill)
- query(): validate_flags() gate before any block extraction; rejects unknown bits,
  conflicting CPU+GPU, and unimplemented GPU path
- query(): validate() called on every decoded TrailEvent after decode
- Added test_header_tamper_detection: flips event_count byte, must fail SHA-256 check
- 18/18 tests pass (was 17/17 + new regression test)

Challenger (GPT-5.4): found 7 criticals; critical #1 pre-fixed by architect StoneEvent wrapper,
criticals #2-4 addressed here. Criticals #5-7 (memory bounds, race/lock, BOM scope) deferred
to documented Phase 2 constraints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement Orphek integration with enhanced device discovery and setup (#1)

* feat: implement Orphek integration with support for OR4-iCon LED Bar

- Add GitHub Actions workflows for pull requests and releases
- Create configuration flow for device setup
- Implement API client for device communication
- Update coordinator and light platform to manage device state
- Enhance documentation and troubleshooting guide
- Update manifest and translation files for new features

* working with tuya app

* Refactor Orphek integration to support Orphek account login and improve device discovery

- Updated config flow to allow login via Orphek account (email/password) instead of Tuya Cloud credentials.
- Implemented new schemas for user input and device discovery.
- Enhanced device discovery to include both LAN and cloud devices, handling local keys more effectively.
- Added new sensor entities for temperature, mode, and schedule information.
- Improved error handling and user feedback during setup.
- Updated documentation strings and translations to reflect changes in setup process.
- Added support for additional features in the light entity, including effects.

* refactor: streamline Orphek login flow and remove unused Tuya integration

* Potential fix for pull request finding

feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool (#4609)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool

Implements roadmap item #219 (v1.9.0):

- Remove per-invocation cudaMalloc/cudaFree from launchHnswSearchKernel();
  caller now provides pre-allocated d_visited pool (nullptr → overflow signal)
- Add persistent visited bitset pool to CudaHnswTraversalEngine::Impl:
  allocated once in buildIndex() at maxBatchSize × ceil(numNodes/8) bytes,
  freed in freeDevice()
- Implement chunked batch processing in batchSearch(): when pool_capacity <
  num_queries, split into sub-batches of pool_capacity, process serially,
  concatenate on host. Covers both single-pass and multi-pass (large-k) paths.
- Expose CudaHnswTraversalEngine::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n) and
  CUDAVectorBackend::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n)
- Clamp pool to 90% of BackendCapabilities::maxMemoryBytes in buildHnswAnnIndex()
- Set BackendHealthStatus::makeDegraded() via setError() when pool allocation
  fails so getHealthStatus() surfaces the issue to callers
- Add 14 new tests: setMaxBatchSize, hasVisitedPool, chunked correctness,
  degraded health on pool failure
- Update FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md and ROADMAP.md to mark item #219 done

commit.message:
Make HTTPS the default; rename flags for clarity

- HTTPS is now the default upstream scheme (no flag needed)
- -insecure: use plain HTTP to upstream (previously required -ssl for https)
- -skip-verify: skip TLS cert verification for self-signed certs
  (previously -insecure)
- -ssl: removed
- Updated usage text, README examples, and log messages

commit.message:
feat: OpenTelemetry observability and production-ready Helm chart (#22)

* feat: add OpenTelemetry observability and production-ready Helm chart

- Introduced a comprehensive design document for integrating OpenTelemetry (OTel) into the AgentLens application.
- Implemented a telemetry package for tracing, metrics, and structured logging.
- Configured a Helm chart with production-grade features including security, scaling, and PostgreSQL support.
- Established observability features such as OTLP exporters, distributed tracing, and Prometheus metrics.
- Added configuration options for telemetry, including environment variable mappings and defaults.
- Defined instrumentation points for HTTP middleware, health probes, parsers, and store operations.
- Created frontend telemetry integration with dynamic configuration delivery.
- Enhanced testing strategy with unit, integration, and Helm tests to ensure observability features function correctly.
- Documented known traps and architectural decisions to guide future development.

* chore(deps): add OpenTelemetry SDK dependencies

* feat(config): add TelemetryConfig with defaults and env var overrides

* feat(telemetry): add Provider Init and Shutdown with OTLP exporters

* feat(telemetry): add fan-out slog handler for stdout + OTLP (ADR-010)

* feat(telemetry): add store tracing decorator for key catalog operations

* feat(telemetry): add health, parser, auth metric instruments and catalog gauge

* feat(api): add /readyz, /metrics, telemetry config handler and otelhttp middleware

- NewReadyzHandler: database liveness probe (pingFn-based)
- NewTelemetryConfigHandler: public endpoint for frontend OTel bootstrap
- RouterDeps: PromHandler, ReadyzPing, TelemetryEnabled/Endpoint/ServiceName fields
- NewRouter return type changed *chi.Mux → http.Handler (server.New already accepts http.Handler)
- otelhttp.NewHandler wraps entire chi mux as outermost handler
- Added go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/net/http/otelhttp v0.68.0

* feat(main): wire telemetry init, fan-out slog, shutdown, readyz, and version ldflags

* feat(health): add OTel tracing and metrics to health prober

* feat(parsers): add OTel tracing and metrics to A2A and MCP parsers

* feat(auth): add OTel span events and metrics to login handler

* feat(main): wire catalog entry gauge async callback

* chore(arch): add telemetry package to infrastructure layer rules

* chore(docker): use distroless nonroot (UID 65532) and inject version at build

* chore(web): add @opentelemetry/* dependencies for trace propagation

* feat(web): add OTel trace propagation with fetch instrumentation

* feat(helm): update Chart.yaml, full values.yaml, multi-replica guard in helpers

* feat(helm): update core templates — deployment, service, SA, configmap, secret

* chore(make): update helm-lint path/flags, add helm-test target

* test(integration): add Docker Compose + Jaeger OTel integration test

* test(e2e): add OTel smoke tests for /readyz, /healthz, telemetry config

* test(helm): add Helm template test script for all value combinations

Adds multi-replica SQLite guard to deployment.yaml to make test 13 pass.
Fixes helm lint -q flag (helm 3.16 uses --quiet).
Updates CLAUDE.md to clarify rtk applies to scripts too.

* fix(telemetry): reduce Init line count and store tracer public functions for arch-go compliance

* fix(arch): reduce recordProbeMetrics params and Login function length for arch-go compliance

* docs: add telemetry config docs, new endpoints, and Helm chart README

- docs/api.md: Document /readyz, /metrics, /api/v1/telemetry/config
- docs/architecture.md: Add Observability section with OTLP diagram
- docs/settings.md: Add Telemetry configuration table
- deploy/helm/agentlens/README.md: Create Helm chart README

* feat(hooks): add script to patch lefthook-generated git hooks for GOPATH support

* docs(claude): make rtk mandatory for ALL commands with explicit examples

* fix(main): wire TracedStore decorator for store tracing spans

* fix(arch): complete telemetry dependency rules — block auth, db, discovery, plugins

* fix(helm): add --config flag to deployment container args

* feat(telemetry): enhance OpenTelemetry configuration and handling

* fix: address remaining review-thread telemetry and helm issues

commit.message:
Initial release of maskproxy

A transparent rewriting reverse proxy in Go that masks an upstream site
from clients by bidirectionally rewriting hostnames and user-defined
string pairs.

Features:
- Target host + all subdomains masked behind proxy address by default
- -exact-domain flag for exact-host-only masking
- Bidirectional string replacement (-replace original:alias,...)
- Case-insensitive replacement (-ci)
- Subdomain routing via /__sd__/ prefix preserving original host
- gzip decompression before body rewrite
- CSP / HSTS / key-pinning headers stripped
- Set-Cookie domain rewrite
- External URL protection (third-party CDN links preserved)
- Body size limit (50 MiB) to prevent OOM
- TLS skip-verify support (-insecure)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: 用 node:22-alpine 重新生成 package-lock.json 修复 Docker npm ci 失败

本地 npm 11 生成的 lockfile 与 Docker node:22-alpine (npm 10) 不兼容，
导致 'Missing: @emnapi/core@1.9.2 from lock file' 错误。
使用 node:22-alpine 容器重新生成 lockfile 解决。

commit.message:
feat(ankh-forge): implement REM Phase 1 trail subsystem (hot/cold/granite stub)

Runestone Execution Model — three-tier typed event trail:
- trail/event.rs: TrailEvent struct with serde + validation
- trail/hot.rs: append, list, verify for .hot.ndjson files
- trail/cold.rs: forge (gzip compress) with round-trip verification
- trail/granite.rs: Phase 2 stub for Vulkan .runestone format
- trail/mod.rs: clap subcommand wiring + trail dir auto-detection

Subcommands: trail append|list|verify|forge
Dependencies added: chrono (serde), flate2

Verified against 19 real events in .chthonic/trail/2026-04-13.hot.ndjson.
All 14 tests pass (11 existing + 3 new).

commit.message:
refactor: make rustttwavvv idiomatic for Rust consumers (#5)

* refactor: make rustttwavvv more idiomatic for Rustaceans

- Add Wav::new() constructor and public accessor methods (format_code, sample_rate, channels, bits, samples, samples_mut, into_samples)
- Add SampleRate::new()/value() and Channels::new()/value() accessors
- Add Bits::byte_count() helper method
- Remove dbg! macros from production code
- Replace verbose Into::<T>::into(x) with idiomatic T::from(x)
- Simplify sample byte parsing: use direct slice operations instead of collecting indexes into intermediate Vecs
- Remove strum dependency: replace EnumIter + LazyLock with const array for SUPPORTED_BITS
- Pre-allocate sample Vec with Vec::with_capacity
- Remove unnecessary type annotations and intermediate variables

commit.message:
Fix/hovmester verify merge queue (#249)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix example generation: use value instead of pointer for model results in response envelopes (#1912)

* fix: example generation uses value instead of pointer for model results in response envelopes

The byValue parameter was hardcoded to false when generating example
response bodies for non-pageable methods, causing model results to be
rendered with & (pointer) syntax. However, modelResult and
polymorphicResult types are anonymously embedded by value in response
structs. The pageable case already correctly used byValue=true.

Fix: compute resultByValue based on the result kind - only
monomorphicResult uses its own byValue property; all other result
types default to by value.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Azure/autorest.go/sessions/27acd500-7ed0-4525-b89f-9df4944a0d8f
